title: Ansible Handlers and Loops
description:
## Notify Once, Loop Many

**Your task:** write a playbook at **`/root/site.yml`** (a YAML list of one play targeting `web`) that:
1. has a task that uses a **`loop`** (e.g. installing several packages)
2. has a task that **`notify`s** a handler
3. defines that handler in a **`handlers`** section

> 💡 Handlers run once, at the end of the play, and only if a task notified them. Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/site.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Play with loop + handler
hints.body: A task notifies a handler by name; the handlers section defines tasks with matching names. loop iterates a list using the item variable.
hints.command:
printf -- '- hosts: web
  tasks:
    - name: install packages
      apt:
        name: "{{ item }}"
        state: present
      loop:
        - nginx
        - curl
    - name: deploy config
      copy:
        dest: /etc/app.conf
        content: "ok"
      notify: restart app
  handlers:
    - name: restart app
      service:
        name: app
        state: restarted
' > /root/site.yml

validation.description: Checks a valid play with handlers/tasks, a notify, and a loop.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/site.yml')); p=d[0]; print('ok' if 'tasks' in p and 'handlers' in p else 'no')"
